title: Embecta expands drug delivery portfolio with US$172.6M Owen Mumford acquisition | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: Owen Mumford brings established expertise in device design, precision moulding, and medical device assembly, which will now be integrated with Embecta’s commercial infrastructure and large-scale m ...
thbAction: Use the article as an entry point to pitch THB's commercial visibility layer for territory planning, account prioritization, and next-best-action for pharma leadership teams.
contentHtml:
UK—Embecta has completed its acquisition of UK-based medical device manufacturer Owen Mumford Holdings in a transaction valued at up to £150 million (US$172.6 million).

The deal, first announced in March 2026, comprises an upfront payment of £100 million (US$115.1 million) and up to £50 million (US$57.5 million) in contingent payments tied to the net sales performance of Owen Mumford’s Aidaptus auto-injector platform over the next three years.

The acquisition marks a significant step in Embecta’s strategy to diversify beyond its established diabetes care business and strengthen its position in advanced drug delivery systems for pharmaceutical partners and chronic disease management.

Expanding focus on chronic and complex conditions

Through this transaction, Embecta is expanding its reach into therapies targeting conditions such as anaphylaxis, obesity, diabetes, and autoimmune diseases.

The company is aligning its product portfolio with the growing global demand for self-administration devices that support long-term treatment outside traditional clinical settings.

By adding Owen Mumford’s technologies, Embecta gains access to a specialised drug-delivery platform that enhances patient convenience and dosing accuracy across multiple therapeutic areas.

In particular, the Aidaptus auto-injector strengthens Embecta’s intellectual property base and broadens its ability to collaborate with pharmaceutical companies developing injectable therapies.

Strengthening device innovation and manufacturing capabilities

Owen Mumford brings established expertise in device design, precision moulding, and medical device assembly, which will now be integrated with Embecta’s commercial infrastructure and large-scale manufacturing capabilities.

The combined organisation is expected to improve production efficiency while expanding global market access for chronic care devices.

Embecta, which employs approximately 2,500 people globally, continues to invest in scalable solutions that support both patients and healthcare providers.

The integration is also expected to enhance operational alignment between design innovation and commercial delivery.

Aidaptus platform and product innovation

At the centre of the acquisition is Owen Mumford’s Aidaptus auto-injector platform, a two-step, single-use subcutaneous device designed for ease of use and adaptability across varying medication volumes.

The system uses a plunger rod insertion mechanism that automatically adjusts to different fill volumes while maintaining a fixed distance between the stopper and plunger.

This design reduces the need for additional verification steps during manufacturing and simplifies final assembly processes.

As a result, it enables faster development cycles for pharmaceutical partners seeking reliable drug-delivery solutions.

Industry momentum and recent developments

In recent company communications, Embecta has continued to emphasise its focus on strengthening partnerships with pharmaceutical innovators and advancing its device portfolio for chronic disease management.

The company has also highlighted ongoing investments in next-generation injection technologies to improve patient adherence and usability in home-based care settings.

Owen Mumford, meanwhile, has maintained its focus on expanding its pipeline of drug delivery and diagnostic devices, with continued emphasis on engineering precision and patient-centric design.

Its platform technologies remain positioned to support future collaborations across injectable therapeutics and combination products.
